name: Sports Management & Leadership
description: An advanced course examining leadership theories and their application in sport organizations. Students develop strategic thinking, organizational management skills, and personal leadership competencies through case studies, simulations, and a capstone leadership development plan.

modules.title: Leadership Theories in Sport
modules.objectives: Identify major leadership theories; compare trait, behavioral, and contingency approaches; analyze how each theory applies to sport organizations.
modules.readingNote: Focus on trait theory, behavioral theory, and contingency models as applied to athletic directors and sport executives.
modules.videoNote: Watch the overview of leadership theory evolution and its relevance to sport contexts.
modules.video2Note: Review situational leadership and how sport managers adapt their style to different team needs.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the leadership styles activity and self-assessment.
modules.assignment: Write a 400-word reflection comparing trait theory and behavioral theory. Identify which theory best describes a sport leader you admire and explain why.

modules.title: Transformational Leadership
modules.objectives: Define transformational leadership; distinguish it from transactional leadership; evaluate transformational behaviors exhibited by iconic sport coaches and executives.
modules.readingNote: Study the four components of transformational leadership—idealized influence, inspirational motivation, intellectual stimulation, and individualized consideration.
modules.videoNote: Watch the transformational vs. transactional leadership comparison lecture.
modules.video2Note: Case study video on transformational coaches in professional sport.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the transformational leadership scenarios practice.
modules.assignment: Select a famous coach or sport executive and write a 500-word analysis demonstrating how they exhibit the four components of transformational leadership.

modules.title: Strategic Planning for Sport Organizations
modules.objectives: Explain the strategic planning process; conduct a SWOT analysis for a sport organization; develop mission, vision, and goal statements.
modules.readingNote: Read about the five-step strategic planning model: environmental scanning, strategy formulation, implementation, evaluation, and control.
modules.videoNote: Watch the strategic planning process tutorial for non-profit and sport organizations.
modules.video2Note: Review SWOT analysis examples from professional sport franchises.
modules.practiceNote: Practice SWOT analysis using the interactive business strategy tool.
modules.assignment: Choose a real sport organization and complete a full SWOT analysis. Write a 500-word strategic summary that proposes two strategic goals based on your findings.

modules.title: Organizational Culture & Team Building
modules.objectives: Define organizational culture; identify elements of strong sport team cultures; design team-building activities that reinforce shared values.
modules.readingNote: Focus on Schein's model of organizational culture and how sport organizations cultivate winning cultures.
modules.videoNote: Watch documentary clips on locker room culture in championship teams.
modules.video2Note: Review strategies for building cohesive organizational culture in sport.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the team dynamics and cohesion exercises.
modules.assignment: Design a three-day team-building retreat for a hypothetical sport organization. Provide an agenda, rationale for each activity, and expected cultural outcomes in a 500-word proposal.

modules.title: Conflict Resolution in Sport Organizations
modules.titleZh: 体育组织中的冲突解决
modules.objectives: Identify sources of conflict in sport organizations; apply Thomas-Kilmann conflict styles; implement negotiation and mediation strategies.
modules.readingNote: Read about the five conflict-handling modes: competing, collaborating, compromising, avoiding, and accommodating.
modules.videoNote: Watch the negotiation and conflict resolution strategies lecture.
modules.video2Note: Case study on labor-management conflicts in professional sport leagues.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the conflict scenario role-play exercises.
modules.assignment: Read a provided case study about a player-coach conflict. Write a 400-word conflict resolution plan identifying the conflict style used, the strategy you would employ, and the expected resolution.

modules.title: Decision-Making in Sport Management
modules.objectives: Apply rational and bounded rationality models to sport decisions; use data to inform management choices; evaluate ethical dimensions of sport management decisions.
modules.readingNote: Study classical decision-making theory, bounded rationality, and heuristics as they apply to player personnel and organizational decisions.
modules.videoNote: Watch the sports analytics and data-driven decision-making lecture.
modules.video2Note: Explore a documentary on the Moneyball approach to sport management decisions.
modules.practiceNote: Work through decision tree exercises applied to sport management scenarios.
modules.assignment: Analyze a major sport organization decision (trade, stadium deal, or coaching change). Write a 500-word critique evaluating the decision process, data used, and whether the outcome matched the analysis.

modules.title: Diversity, Equity & Inclusion in Sport
modules.titleZh: 体育中的多元、公平与包容
modules.objectives: Examine current diversity challenges in sport leadership; evaluate inclusion policies in professional leagues; develop strategies to promote equity in sport organizations.
modules.readingNote: Read about the Rooney Rule, gender equity in athletics, and racial equity in sport leadership hiring.
modules.videoNote: Watch the diversity and inclusion in sport leadership panel discussion.
modules.video2Note: Review case studies on Title IX enforcement and gender equity outcomes.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the DEI self-assessment and organizational audit activity.
modules.assignment: Write a 500-word diversity action plan for a university athletic department. Include three specific, measurable initiatives targeting hiring, programming, and culture change.

modules.title: Financial Strategy for Sport Organizations
modules.objectives: Interpret sport organization financial statements; evaluate revenue diversification strategies; develop a basic budget for a sport program.
modules.readingNote: Study revenue streams, cost management, salary cap structures, and endowment strategies used by professional and collegiate sport organizations.
modules.videoNote: Watch the sport finance fundamentals lecture covering revenue models and expense management.
modules.video2Note: Review how NFL and NBA teams manage salary caps as a financial strategy tool.
modules.practiceNote: Practice reading and analyzing sample sport organization financial statements.
modules.assignment: Create a one-year budget for a high school athletics department with a $200,000 operating budget. Justify your allocations in a 400-word memo to a hypothetical athletic director.

modules.title: Community Relations & Social Responsibility
modules.objectives: Define corporate social responsibility in sport; evaluate community outreach programs; design a CSR initiative for a sport organization.
modules.readingNote: Read about how professional teams use foundations, player community programs, and stadium initiatives to create positive community impact.
modules.videoNote: Watch a case study on how the NBA Cares program operates and measures community impact.
modules.video2Note: Explore how Premier League clubs run grassroots community sports programs.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the CSR planning and stakeholder mapping activity.
modules.assignment: Develop a CSR program concept for a professional sport team in your city. Write a 500-word proposal outlining the program's mission, target community, activities, and measurement metrics.

modules.title: Crisis Management in Sport
modules.objectives: Identify types of crises affecting sport organizations; apply crisis communication principles; develop a crisis response plan for a sport organization.
modules.readingNote: Study crisis typology, the three-stage crisis model (pre-crisis, crisis, post-crisis), and Coombs's Situational Crisis Communication Theory.
modules.videoNote: Watch the crisis communication in sport lecture covering real organizational crises.
modules.video2Note: Case study on how the NFL handled the concussion crisis—a communications and management analysis.
modules.practiceNote: Work through crisis scenario simulations and draft a crisis response statement.
modules.assignment: Develop a crisis communication plan for a hypothetical doping scandal involving a star athlete. Include crisis type, stakeholder messaging, communication timeline, and recovery strategy in 500 words.

modules.title: Sport Innovation & Technology
modules.objectives: Assess how technology is transforming sport management; evaluate wearable tech, data analytics, and fan engagement platforms; propose an innovative technology solution for a sport organization.
modules.readingNote: Read about the role of AI, big data, wearables, and esports in reshaping the modern sport industry.
modules.videoNote: Watch the sport technology innovation lecture covering data analytics and performance tracking tools.
modules.video2Note: Explore how esports organizations use technology for fan engagement and revenue generation.
modules.practiceNote: Complete the sport technology landscape analysis activity.
modules.assignment: Write a 500-word innovation proposal for introducing a technology solution (e.g., AI-powered scouting, fan engagement app, smart stadium) to a sport organization. Include cost-benefit considerations.

quizQuestions.question: Which leadership theory focuses on identifying innate characteristics that make individuals effective leaders?
options: Situational leadership theory
Trait theory
Path-goal theory
Contingency theory
quizQuestions.answer: Trait theory
quizQuestions.explanation: Trait theory holds that certain innate qualities—such as intelligence, charisma, and decisiveness—distinguish leaders from non-leaders.

quizQuestions.question: The contingency approach to leadership suggests that effective leadership depends on:
options: A single universal style that works in all situations
The match between a leader's style and situational variables
Following strict hierarchical authority structures
Rewarding followers with transactional incentives
quizQuestions.answer: The match between a leader's style and situational variables
quizQuestions.explanation: Contingency theory argues that no single leadership style is best; effectiveness depends on how well the leader's style fits the situation.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which leadership theory focuses on identifying innate characteristics that make individuals effective leaders" is: Trait theory.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Trait theory is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Fill in the blank: Complete: In the context of the topic, ___ refers to "in sport management, which theory is most useful for understanding how an athletic director should adjust leadership style when managing both experienced coaches and new staff."
quizQuestions.answer: Situational leadership theory
quizQuestions.explanation: The correct answer is: Situational leadership theory.

quizQuestions.question: Which component of transformational leadership involves a leader serving as an ethical role model?
options: Intellectual stimulation
Individualized consideration
Idealized influence
Inspirational motivation
quizQuestions.answer: Idealized influence
quizQuestions.explanation: Idealized influence refers to leaders who act as role models by demonstrating high ethical standards and earning followers' admiration and trust.

quizQuestions.question: Transactional leadership primarily motivates followers through:
options: Inspiring a shared vision for the future
Contingent rewards and corrective actions
Providing personalized developmental support
Challenging followers to think creatively
quizQuestions.answer: Contingent rewards and corrective actions
quizQuestions.explanation: Transactional leadership uses exchanges—rewards for performance and corrections for deficiencies—to manage follower behavior.

quizQuestions.question: A basketball coach who encourages players to question established training methods is demonstrating which transformational component?
options: Idealized influence
Inspirational motivation
Intellectual stimulation
Individualized consideration
quizQuestions.answer: Intellectual stimulation
quizQuestions.explanation: Intellectual stimulation involves encouraging followers to challenge assumptions and think creatively to solve problems.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Which component of transformational leadership involves a leader serving as an ethical role model" is: Idealized influence.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Idealized influence is correct.

quizQuestions.question: In strategic planning, a SWOT analysis assesses:
options: Sales, wages, operations, and talent
Speed, width, outreach, and technology
Strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats
Systems, workflow, objectives, and timelines
quizQuestions.answer: Strengths, weaknesses, opportunities, and threats
quizQuestions.explanation: SWOT analysis is a strategic tool that examines internal strengths and weaknesses alongside external opportunities and threats.

quizQuestions.question: The mission statement of a sport organization should:
options: Describe the organization's financial performance targets
Articulate the organization's purpose and reason for existence
List all tactical activities for the coming year
Define the legal structure of the organization
quizQuestions.answer: Articulate the organization's purpose and reason for existence
quizQuestions.explanation: A mission statement defines why an organization exists—its core purpose, who it serves, and what it does.

quizQuestions.question: Environmental scanning in strategic planning refers to:
options: Auditing a stadium's physical environment for safety hazards
Monitoring and analyzing internal and external factors affecting the organization
Designing green sustainability initiatives for sport facilities
Reviewing financial spreadsheets for budget variances
quizQuestions.answer: Monitoring and analyzing internal and external factors affecting the organization
quizQuestions.explanation: Environmental scanning involves systematically collecting and analyzing information about internal operations and external trends to inform strategy.

quizQuestions.question: According to Edgar Schein, which level of organizational culture is most difficult to change?
options: Artifacts and symbols
Espoused values
Basic underlying assumptions
Behavioral norms and rules
quizQuestions.answer: Basic underlying assumptions
quizQuestions.explanation: Schein's model identifies basic underlying assumptions—unconscious, taken-for-granted beliefs—as the deepest and most resistant-to-change level of culture.

quizQuestions.question: Team cohesion in sport organizations refers to:
options: Individual performance statistics of team members
The tendency for a group to stick together and remain united in pursuit of goals
The formal hierarchy within an athletic department
Competitive rivalry among team members for playing time
quizQuestions.answer: The tendency for a group to stick together and remain united in pursuit of goals
quizQuestions.explanation: Team cohesion describes the dynamic process that keeps group members together and committed to shared goals.

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is an example of a cultural artifact in a sport organization?
options: The underlying belief that winning justifies any means
An unspoken assumption about gender roles in coaching
Team trophies, championship banners, and team rituals
The private values held by individual athletes
quizQuestions.answer: Team trophies, championship banners, and team rituals
quizQuestions.explanation: Artifacts are the visible, tangible elements of culture—physical symbols, rituals, and stories that reflect the organization's values.

quizQuestions.question: Task cohesion in sport teams is best described as:
options: The degree to which team members socialize outside of games
The commitment of team members to working together to achieve a common goal
Personal friendships among athletes on a team
The loyalty players feel toward the team's owner
quizQuestions.answer: The commitment of team members to working together to achieve a common goal
quizQuestions.explanation: Task cohesion refers specifically to how united team members are in their pursuit of the group's performance objectives.

quizQuestions.question: According to the Thomas-Kilmann model, which conflict style involves high assertiveness and high cooperativeness?
options: Avoiding
Competing
Compromising
Collaborating
quizQuestions.answer: Collaborating
quizQuestions.explanation: Collaborating is the win-win style that is both highly assertive (pursuing your own goals) and highly cooperative (attending to others' concerns).

quizQuestions.question: A sport manager who delays addressing a conflict between two coaches is using which conflict style?
options: Collaborating
Competing
Avoiding
Accommodating
quizQuestions.answer: Avoiding
quizQuestions.explanation: Avoiding involves sidestepping or postponing a conflict, characterized by low assertiveness and low cooperativeness.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "According to the Thomas-Kilmann model, which conflict style involves high assertiveness and high cooperativeness" is: Collaborating.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Collaborating is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "A sport manager who delays addressing a conflict between two coaches is using which conflict style" is: Collaborating.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: False
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Avoiding.

quizQuestions.question: The rational decision-making model assumes that decision-makers:
options: Are limited by cognitive biases and incomplete information
Act on emotion rather than data in high-pressure situations
Have access to complete information and make optimal choices
Rely on past experience rather than logical analysis
quizQuestions.answer: Have access to complete information and make optimal choices
quizQuestions.explanation: The rational model assumes full information, clear preferences, and the ability to identify the optimal solution—conditions rarely met in practice.

quizQuestions.question: Herbert Simon's concept of 'bounded rationality' means that decision-makers:
options: Always make perfectly optimal decisions given enough time
Are constrained by limited information, cognitive capacity, and time
Use only emotional judgment when making decisions
Delegate all decisions to algorithms and data systems
quizQuestions.answer: Are constrained by limited information, cognitive capacity, and time
quizQuestions.explanation: Bounded rationality acknowledges that humans 'satisfice'—making good-enough decisions given real cognitive and information constraints.

quizQuestions.question: The Moneyball approach to sport management decision-making relies primarily on:
options: Gut instinct of experienced scouts
Statistical analysis and sabermetrics to evaluate player value
Owner preferences and fan popularity of players
Established scouting traditions and subjective assessments
quizQuestions.answer: Statistical analysis and sabermetrics to evaluate player value
quizQuestions.explanation: The Moneyball approach, pioneered by the Oakland A's, uses objective statistical metrics to identify undervalued players and make personnel decisions.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The rational decision-making model assumes that decision-makers:" is: Have access to complete information and make optimal choices.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Have access to complete information and make optimal choices is correct.

quizQuestions.question: The NFL's Rooney Rule requires teams to:
options: Pay a minimum salary to all practice squad players
Interview at least one minority candidate for head coaching and senior football operations positions
Dedicate a percentage of revenue to community programs
Report concussion incidents to the league within 24 hours
quizQuestions.answer: Interview at least one minority candidate for head coaching and senior football operations positions
quizQuestions.explanation: The Rooney Rule mandates that NFL teams interview at least one minority candidate for head coaching and senior front office positions to increase diversity in leadership.

quizQuestions.question: Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 requires that:
options: All college athletes receive academic tutoring support
No person be discriminated against based on sex in federally funded education programs
Athletic scholarships be limited to academic merit only
Schools report all athletic injuries to the federal government
quizQuestions.answer: No person be discriminated against based on sex in federally funded education programs
quizQuestions.explanation: Title IX prohibits sex discrimination in any educational program or activity receiving federal financial assistance, significantly impacting collegiate athletics.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "The NFL's Rooney Rule requires teams to:" is: Interview at least one minority candidate for head coaching and senior football operations positions.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Interview at least one minority candidate for head coaching and senior football operations positions is correct.

quizQuestions.question: In professional sport, a salary cap is designed to:
options: Guarantee minimum player salaries
Limit the total amount teams can spend on player salaries to promote competitive balance
Set a maximum ticket price for fans
Regulate the number of international players on a roster
quizQuestions.answer: Limit the total amount teams can spend on player salaries to promote competitive balance
quizQuestions.explanation: Salary caps restrict total team payroll to prevent wealthy franchises from dominating player acquisition and to maintain competitive balance across the league.

quizQuestions.question: Revenue diversification in sport organizations refers to:
options: Focusing all income generation on ticket sales
Generating income from multiple sources to reduce financial risk
Distributing revenue equally to all sport programs
Limiting spending to a single budget category
quizQuestions.answer: Generating income from multiple sources to reduce financial risk
quizQuestions.explanation: Revenue diversification spreads income across sources such as gate receipts, broadcasting, licensing, sponsorships, and concessions to reduce dependence on any single stream.

quizQuestions.question: Which financial statement shows a sport organization's revenues and expenses over a specific time period?
options: Balance sheet
Cash flow statement
Income statement
Equity statement
quizQuestions.answer: Income statement
quizQuestions.explanation: The income statement (profit and loss statement) summarizes revenues and expenses over a defined period, showing the organization's net income or loss.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "In professional sport, a salary cap is designed to:" is: Limit the total amount teams can spend on player salaries to promote competitive balance.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Limit the total amount teams can spend on player salaries to promote competitive balance is correct.

quizQuestions.question: Corporate social responsibility (CSR) in sport organizations refers to:
options: A company's legal obligations to government regulators
Voluntary initiatives to have a positive social and environmental impact
Public relations campaigns to increase ticket sales
Annual tax filings required by nonprofit organizations
quizQuestions.answer: Voluntary initiatives to have a positive social and environmental impact
quizQuestions.explanation: CSR involves organizations voluntarily taking actions that benefit society and the environment beyond what is legally required.

quizQuestions.question: Stakeholder theory in CSR suggests that organizations should be accountable to:
options: Only their shareholders and investors
Government regulatory agencies exclusively
All groups who are affected by or can affect the organization
Only the athletes who represent the organization
quizQuestions.answer: All groups who are affected by or can affect the organization
quizQuestions.explanation: Stakeholder theory holds that organizations must consider the interests of all parties affected by their decisions, including employees, customers, communities, and shareholders.

quizQuestions.question: The NBA Cares program is an example of professional sport leagues using:
options: Revenue sharing to improve competitive balance
Community outreach to create positive social impact and enhance brand image
Financial aid for retired players with medical needs
Educational scholarships for front office employees
quizQuestions.answer: Community outreach to create positive social impact and enhance brand image
quizQuestions.explanation: NBA Cares is the league's global social responsibility initiative, uniting players, teams, and staff in addressing social issues and strengthening communities.

quizQuestions.question: Coombs's Situational Crisis Communication Theory (SCCT) categorizes crises based on:
options: The size of the organization experiencing the crisis
The level of organizational crisis responsibility attributed by stakeholders
The media outlet that first reports the crisis
The financial cost of the crisis to the organization
quizQuestions.answer: The level of organizational crisis responsibility attributed by stakeholders
quizQuestions.explanation: SCCT classifies crises by the amount of responsibility stakeholders attribute to the organization, which determines the appropriate crisis communication response strategy.

quizQuestions.question: Which crisis communication strategy involves an organization accepting full responsibility and apologizing to affected parties?
options: Denial strategy
Diminishment strategy
Bolstering strategy
Rebuild/mortification strategy
quizQuestions.answer: Rebuild/mortification strategy
quizQuestions.explanation: The rebuild strategy (including mortification/apology) is used when an organization accepts crisis responsibility and seeks to repair relationships with affected stakeholders.

quizQuestions.question: The pre-crisis phase of crisis management primarily involves:
options: Responding to media inquiries after an incident occurs
Conducting post-crisis evaluation and recovery
Prevention, planning, and training before a crisis occurs
Distributing blame to minimize organizational liability
quizQuestions.answer: Prevention, planning, and training before a crisis occurs
quizQuestions.explanation: The pre-crisis phase focuses on identifying risks, developing crisis management plans, and training spokespersons before any crisis materializes.

quizQuestions.question: Which crisis type typically generates the highest reputational damage for sport organizations?
options: Natural disaster affecting a venue
Equipment malfunction during a game
Ethical misconduct or cover-up by leadership
Scheduling conflicts due to weather
quizQuestions.answer: Ethical misconduct or cover-up by leadership
quizQuestions.explanation: Intentional wrongdoing and cover-ups generate the most reputational damage because stakeholders attribute high organizational responsibility and see the behavior as preventable.

quizQuestions.question: Wearable technology in sport performance management is primarily used to:
options: Replace the role of coaches entirely
Monitor athlete biometrics and workload to optimize training and prevent injury
Broadcast games to streaming platforms
Track ticket sales and fan attendance
quizQuestions.answer: Monitor athlete biometrics and workload to optimize training and prevent injury
quizQuestions.explanation: Wearable technology collects real-time physiological data (heart rate, GPS, acceleration) to inform training decisions and reduce injury risk.

quizQuestions.question: In sport management, 'big data' refers to:
options: Large stadium capacities and fan attendance figures
Vast volumes of structured and unstructured data used for analysis and decision-making
Financial data from league revenue reports only
Media coverage statistics tracked by public relations teams
quizQuestions.answer: Vast volumes of structured and unstructured data used for analysis and decision-making
quizQuestions.explanation: Big data in sport encompasses massive datasets from performance tracking, social media, ticketing, and fan engagement used to gain competitive and business insights.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "Wearable technology in sport performance management is primarily used to:" is: Monitor athlete biometrics and workload to optimize training and prevent injury.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: True
quizQuestions.explanation: True. Monitor athlete biometrics and workload to optimize training and prevent injury is correct.

quizQuestions.question: True or False: The answer to "In sport management, 'big data' refers to:" is: Large stadium capacities and fan attendance figures.
options: True
False
quizQuestions.answer: False
quizQuestions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Vast volumes of structured and unstructured data used for analysis and decision-making.

questions.question: Which leadership theory proposes that effectiveness depends on matching a leader's style to the demands of the situation?
options: Trait theory
Contingency theory
Path-goal theory
Great Man theory
questions.answer: Contingency theory
questions.explanation: Contingency theory argues that leadership effectiveness depends on the fit between leader style and situational factors.

questions.question: Transformational leaders inspire followers primarily by:
options: Offering bonuses and financial rewards for performance
Articulating a compelling vision that appeals to followers' values and aspirations
Enforcing strict rules and monitoring compliance closely
Delegating all decisions to autonomous teams
questions.answer: Articulating a compelling vision that appeals to followers' values and aspirations
questions.explanation: Transformational leaders motivate through inspirational motivation—sharing an exciting, value-laden vision that elevates followers' goals.

questions.question: In a SWOT analysis, 'opportunities' refers to:
options: Internal capabilities that give the organization an advantage
Internal weaknesses that need to be corrected
External conditions that the organization can exploit for benefit
External threats that could harm the organization
questions.answer: External conditions that the organization can exploit for benefit
questions.explanation: Opportunities in SWOT are favorable external conditions—market trends, gaps, or changes the organization can leverage.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "The deepest and most difficult-to-change level of organizational culture, according to Schein, is:" is: Basic underlying assumptions.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Basic underlying assumptions is correct.

questions.question: In the Thomas-Kilmann model, the collaborating conflict style is characterized by:
options: Low assertiveness and high cooperativeness
Low assertiveness and low cooperativeness
High assertiveness and low cooperativeness
High assertiveness and high cooperativeness
questions.answer: High assertiveness and high cooperativeness
questions.explanation: Collaborating combines strong concern for both one's own outcomes and the other party's outcomes, making it the most integrative conflict style.

questions.question: A sport manager who satisfices rather than optimizes is demonstrating:
options: Perfect rationality in decision-making
Bounded rationality
Transformational decision-making
Zero-based decision analysis
questions.answer: Bounded rationality
questions.explanation: Satisficing—choosing a good-enough option rather than the optimal one—is the hallmark of Simon's bounded rationality concept.

questions.question: The four components of Bass's transformational leadership model include all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.answer: Contingent reward
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Contingent reward.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A sport organization's mission statement differs from its strategic goals in that the mission:" is: Is updated annually with specific financial targets.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Describes the enduring purpose of the organization, not specific actions.

questions.question: Task cohesion in sport teams is most directly associated with:
options: Social bonding and friendship among athletes
Shared commitment to achieving the team's performance goals
Satisfaction with equipment and facility quality
Individual athletes' relationship with the head coach
questions.answer: Shared commitment to achieving the team's performance goals
questions.explanation: Task cohesion reflects how unified team members are in pursuing collective performance outcomes.

questions.question: Integrative negotiation differs from distributive negotiation in that integrative negotiation:
options: Focuses on winning at the other party's expense
Seeks to create value and find mutually beneficial solutions
Is used only in collective bargaining situations
Avoids any compromise between parties
questions.answer: Seeks to create value and find mutually beneficial solutions
questions.explanation: Integrative negotiation looks for solutions that expand value for both parties rather than dividing a fixed set of resources.

questions.question: The Rooney Rule in the NFL was established to address:
options: Financial fairness in player contract negotiations
Lack of diversity in coaching and senior management hiring
Excessive player fines and disciplinary inequality
International player eligibility restrictions
questions.answer: Lack of diversity in coaching and senior management hiring
questions.explanation: The Rooney Rule was implemented to increase opportunities for minority candidates in NFL head coaching and senior leadership positions.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Title IX had the most direct impact on collegiate athletics by:" is: Requiring equal participation opportunities and resource allocation for female athletes.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Requiring equal participation opportunities and resource allocation for female athletes is correct.

questions.question: The 'glass ceiling' metaphor in sport leadership specifically describes:
options: Physical barriers in stadium design preventing fan access
Transparent financial reporting requirements for sport organizations
Invisible systemic barriers preventing women and minorities from reaching top leadership positions
The competitive plateau experienced by high-performing athletes
questions.answer: Invisible systemic barriers preventing women and minorities from reaching top leadership positions
questions.explanation: The glass ceiling refers to unseen organizational and societal barriers that prevent qualified women and minorities from advancing to executive leadership.

questions.question: Groupthink is most likely to occur in sport management teams that:
options: Regularly invite external perspectives and dissenting views
Prioritize group harmony and consensus over critical evaluation
Use structured devil's advocate processes in decision-making
Have high turnover and constantly changing membership
questions.answer: Prioritize group harmony and consensus over critical evaluation
questions.explanation: Groupthink thrives in highly cohesive groups where conformity pressure suppresses dissent and critical analysis.

questions.question: Data-driven decision-making in sport management, exemplified by the Moneyball approach, relies on:
options: Traditional scouting intuition as the primary decision tool
Statistical analysis to identify and acquire undervalued talent and make personnel decisions
Media popularity and fan preference surveys for roster decisions
Coaching experience and gut instinct over objective metrics
questions.answer: Statistical analysis to identify and acquire undervalued talent and make personnel decisions
questions.explanation: The Moneyball philosophy uses objective statistical analysis (sabermetrics) to make player acquisition decisions based on performance value rather than conventional wisdom.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Situational leadership theory prescribes that leaders should increase directing behavior when:" is: Followers have high competence and high commitment.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Followers have low competence and low commitment.

questions.question: Intellectual stimulation as a component of transformational leadership refers to a leader who:
options: Provides material rewards for creative performance
Monitors and corrects follower performance against set standards
Encourages followers to question assumptions and think innovatively
Maintains close personal relationships with all team members
questions.answer: Encourages followers to question assumptions and think innovatively
questions.explanation: Intellectual stimulation involves challenging followers to reconsider problems from new angles and to develop creative solutions.

questions.question: Environmental scanning in strategic planning typically examines which external factors?
options: Internal budget variances and departmental performance
Only competitor strategies and market pricing
Political, economic, social, technological, and competitive forces
The personal development goals of front office staff
questions.answer: Political, economic, social, technological, and competitive forces
questions.explanation: Environmental scanning uses frameworks like PESTEL to analyze the external environment—political, economic, social, technological, environmental, and legal forces.

questions.question: Social cohesion in sport teams differs from task cohesion in that social cohesion reflects:
options: Commitment to team performance goals and strategies
Interpersonal attraction and friendship among team members
Agreement about individual roles and responsibilities
Shared confidence in the team's ability to perform
questions.answer: Interpersonal attraction and friendship among team members
questions.explanation: Social cohesion refers to the quality of interpersonal relationships within a team, while task cohesion focuses on collective commitment to goals.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "A sport manager using the accommodating conflict style is demonstrating:" is: Low assertiveness and high cooperativeness.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Low assertiveness and high cooperativeness is correct.

questions.question: Corporate social responsibility in sport organizations is described as 'voluntary' because it:
options: Is funded entirely by employee volunteers
Goes beyond legal requirements to create positive social and environmental impact
Is only practiced by nonprofit sport organizations
Is mandated by the IOC but not enforced
questions.answer: Goes beyond legal requirements to create positive social and environmental impact
questions.explanation: CSR is inherently voluntary—organizations choose to take actions for social benefit beyond what laws require.

questions.question: Coombs's Situational Crisis Communication Theory recommends that organizations facing crises attributed to them use which communication strategy?
options: Denial—claiming the crisis did not occur
Diminishment—minimizing the severity of the crisis
Bolstering—reminding stakeholders of past positive actions
Rebuild—taking responsibility and offering compensation
questions.answer: Rebuild—taking responsibility and offering compensation
questions.explanation: When organizations bear high responsibility for a crisis, SCCT recommends rebuild strategies including apology and corrective action.

questions.question: Which financial metric represents the difference between total revenue and total expenses for a sport organization?
options: Return on investment
Net income or net loss
Operating cash flow
Debt-to-equity ratio
questions.answer: Net income or net loss
questions.explanation: Net income (or net loss) is the bottom line of the income statement, calculated as total revenues minus total expenses.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "AI-powered analytics in sport management is most commonly applied to:" is: Automating referee decisions during competition.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Identifying performance patterns and predicting injury risk in athlete data.

questions.question: In the GROW coaching model, the 'O' stage involves:
options: Setting the goal for the coaching session
Assessing the current reality and challenges
Generating possible options and solutions
Committing to a specific will and action plan
questions.answer: Generating possible options and solutions
questions.explanation: The Options stage of the GROW model involves brainstorming and exploring different approaches the coachee could take to address their challenge.

questions.question: Cross-cultural leadership intelligence (CQ) in global sport management involves the ability to:
options: Enforce a uniform organizational culture across all international branches
Adapt one's leadership style and behavior effectively across different cultural contexts
Hire only domestic employees to avoid cultural complexity
Translate all organizational communications into multiple languages
questions.answer: Adapt one's leadership style and behavior effectively across different cultural contexts
questions.explanation: Cultural intelligence (CQ) is the capability to function effectively in a variety of cultural contexts by adapting one's thinking and behavior.

questions.question: A leadership development plan differs from a career plan in that it specifically focuses on:
options: Projected salary increases and job titles over time
Competency growth, behavior change, and leadership skill development
Academic qualifications and degree programs to pursue
Networking contacts and professional association memberships
questions.answer: Competency growth, behavior change, and leadership skill development
questions.explanation: A leadership development plan targets specific leadership competencies and behaviors to develop, not just career trajectory milestones.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "Equity-focused leadership in sport organizations requires leaders to:" is: Recognize systemic disadvantages and allocate resources accordingly to level the playing field.
options: True
False
questions.answer: True
questions.explanation: True. Recognize systemic disadvantages and allocate resources accordingly to level the playing field is correct.

questions.question: The post-crisis phase of crisis management primarily involves:
options: Activating emergency response protocols for the first time
Preventing the crisis through risk assessment and training
Evaluating the crisis response, repairing relationships, and implementing corrective actions
Issuing the first public statement about the crisis
questions.answer: Evaluating the crisis response, repairing relationships, and implementing corrective actions
questions.explanation: Post-crisis management involves reviewing the effectiveness of the response, rebuilding stakeholder trust, and making organizational improvements.

questions.question: Social Return on Investment (SROI) is a measurement tool used by sport organizations to:
options: Calculate ticket revenue per seat in a stadium
Quantify the broader social, environmental, and economic value of CSR programs
Measure the return on social media marketing investments
Assess the financial return of player salary investments
questions.answer: Quantify the broader social, environmental, and economic value of CSR programs
questions.explanation: SROI measures the social value generated by an investment—accounting for benefits to communities and stakeholders beyond pure financial returns.

questions.question: Reverse mentoring is particularly valuable in sport organizations because it:
options: Replaces the need for formal leadership development programs
Allows senior leaders to teach junior employees executive management skills
Gives senior leaders insights into technology, social media, and emerging cultural perspectives from younger employees
Creates a hierarchical accountability structure for performance management
questions.answer: Gives senior leaders insights into technology, social media, and emerging cultural perspectives from younger employees
questions.explanation: Reverse mentoring bridges the generational knowledge gap by having younger employees share their expertise with senior leaders, particularly about technology and culture.

questions.question: True or False: The answer to "WADA's role in global sport governance is primarily to:" is: Organize international sport competitions and award Olympic medals.
options: True
False
questions.answer: False
questions.explanation: False. The correct answer is: Establish and enforce consistent anti-doping standards across international sport.

questions.question: A personal leadership philosophy is most useful to a sport management professional because it:
options: Provides a step-by-step decision algorithm for all management situations
Serves as an enduring moral compass guiding decisions under pressure and ambiguity
Outlines specific financial targets for career advancement
Replaces the need for formal leadership training and coaching
questions.answer: Serves as an enduring moral compass guiding decisions under pressure and ambiguity
questions.explanation: A personal leadership philosophy articulates core values and principles that guide behavior consistently, especially in challenging and ambiguous situations.

questions.question: Zero-based budgeting requires sport managers to:
options: Maintain the previous year's budget without modification
Begin each budget cycle by justifying all expenditures from zero
Cut 20% from all budget lines to control costs
Allocate funds only after reviewing competitor spending
questions.answer: Begin each budget cycle by justifying all expenditures from zero
questions.explanation: Zero-based budgeting starts from a 'zero base'—every function and expense must be justified anew rather than using prior-year allocations as a baseline.

questions.question: The capstone leadership development plan integrates course learning by requiring students to:
options: Memorize and recite major leadership theories from the course
Synthesize theory and self-reflection into a personalized, actionable leadership growth plan
Critique the leadership failures of famous sport executives
Write a comprehensive history of leadership in sport management
questions.answer: Synthesize theory and self-reflection into a personalized, actionable leadership growth plan
questions.explanation: The capstone plan requires students to integrate leadership theory with personal self-assessment to create an actionable plan for their own development as future sport leaders.
